WilliamEtAl_Dec2025_GRSPfood

Emily Williams, Theo Michaels, Alice Boyle

04 December 2025

true

Datasets are used for this analysis:

  • “EdibleMassData2014_raw.csv”
  • “GRSP_Food_Manuscript_Final_Dec2025.csv”
  • “GRSP_DispersalData_withBFE_April2025.csv”

PREDICTION I ANALYSIS

Prediction I: Food abundance would vary temporally over single breeding seasons, and spatially in response to the individual and interactive disturbances of burning and grazing.

##########################################################
######################### STEP 1 #########################
##########################################################

# BRING IN DATA  ------------------------
df_Food_og <- read.csv("EdibleMassData2014_raw.csv")

# ASSIGN VARIABLES STRUCTURE AS NEEDED ------------------------
df_Food_og$YrsSinceBurn <- as.factor(df_Food_og$YrsSinceBurn)
df_Food_og$GrazingTreatment <- as.factor(df_Food_og$GrazingTreatment)
df_Food_og$Watershed <- as.factor(df_Food_og$Watershed)
df_Food_og$TimePeriod <- as.factor(df_Food_og$TimePeriod)

# Fix zLate to Late
levels(df_Food_og$TimePeriod)[levels(df_Food_og$TimePeriod) == "zLate"]<-"Late"

# SUMMARIZE TRANSECTS SECTIONS ------------------------

df_Food<-df_Food_og %>%
  group_by(Watershed, TransectID, TimePeriod, BurnTreatment, GrazingTreatment, YrsSinceBurn) %>%
   summarize(EdibleMass = sum(EdibleMass)) %>%
  as.data.frame()
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Watershed', 'TransectID', 'TimePeriod',
## 'BurnTreatment', 'GrazingTreatment'. You can override using the `.groups`
## argument.
# TRANSFORMATIONS ------------------------

df_Food$EdibleMass_trans<-(log(df_Food$EdibleMass))


##########################################################
######################### STEP 2 #########################
##########################################################

# SUMMARY OF EDIABLE BIOMASS BY TIME PERIOD ------------------------
df_EBbyTP_SummaryStats<- df_Food %>%
group_by(TimePeriod) %>%
    summarise(
    EB_mean = mean(EdibleMass, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

##########################################################
######################### STEP 3 #########################
##########################################################

# MODELS FOR PREDICTION 1 ------------------------

# Mod_01: additive ------------------------
mod_Food_01 <- lmer(EdibleMass_trans ~ TimePeriod + GrazingTreatment + YrsSinceBurn + (1|Watershed) + (1|Watershed:TransectID), data = df_Food, REML=FALSE)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
summary(mod_Food_01)
## Linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood . t-tests use Satterthwaite's
##   method [lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: EdibleMass_trans ~ TimePeriod + GrazingTreatment + YrsSinceBurn +  
##     (1 | Watershed) + (1 | Watershed:TransectID)
##    Data: df_Food
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     328.7     359.6    -154.3     308.7       152 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.8533 -0.6451  0.0433  0.6220  2.2012 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups               Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Watershed:TransectID (Intercept) 0.0000   0.0000  
##  Watershed            (Intercept) 0.1326   0.3641  
##  Residual                         0.3323   0.5764  
## Number of obs: 162, groups:  Watershed:TransectID, 54; Watershed, 18
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                         Estimate Std. Error        df t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)              0.83191    0.22386  21.33143   3.716  0.00125 ** 
## TimePeriodMid            1.10250    0.11093 144.00000   9.938  < 2e-16 ***
## TimePeriodLate           1.47921    0.11093 144.00000  13.334  < 2e-16 ***
## GrazingTreatmentCattle   0.02672    0.26652  18.00000   0.100  0.92126    
## GrazingTreatmentNone    -0.12321    0.26652  18.00000  -0.462  0.64943    
## YrsSinceBurn1           -0.37272    0.24108  18.00000  -1.546  0.13949    
## YrsSinceBurn2           -0.68599    0.34564  18.00000  -1.985  0.06264 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##             (Intr) TmPrdM TmPrdL GrznTC GrznTN YrsSB1
## TimePeridMd -0.248                                   
## TimePeridLt -0.248  0.500                            
## GrzngTrtmnC -0.690  0.000  0.000                     
## GrzngTrtmnN -0.731  0.000  0.000  0.591              
## YrsSincBrn1 -0.269  0.000  0.000 -0.075  0.075       
## YrsSincBrn2 -0.063  0.000  0.000 -0.324  0.018  0.232
## optimizer (nloptwrap) convergence code: 0 (OK)
## boundary (singular) fit: see help('isSingular')
# Mod_02: interactions  ------------------------
mod_Food_02 <- lmer(EdibleMass_trans ~ TimePeriod + GrazingTreatment + YrsSinceBurn + TimePeriod*GrazingTreatment + TimePeriod*YrsSinceBurn + (1|Watershed) + (1|Watershed:TransectID), data = df_Food, REML=FALSE)
summary(mod_Food_02)
## Linear mixed model fit by maximum likelihood . t-tests use Satterthwaite's
##   method [lmerModLmerTest]
## Formula: EdibleMass_trans ~ TimePeriod + GrazingTreatment + YrsSinceBurn +  
##     TimePeriod * GrazingTreatment + TimePeriod * YrsSinceBurn +  
##     (1 | Watershed) + (1 | Watershed:TransectID)
##    Data: df_Food
## 
##       AIC       BIC    logLik -2*log(L)  df.resid 
##     306.3     361.9    -135.2     270.3       144 
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -3.09213 -0.61868  0.01291  0.66459  2.25310 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups               Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  Watershed:TransectID (Intercept) 0.0182   0.1349  
##  Watershed            (Intercept) 0.1366   0.3696  
##  Residual                         0.2419   0.4919  
## Number of obs: 162, groups:  Watershed:TransectID, 54; Watershed, 18
## 
## Fixed effects:
##                                        Estimate Std. Error        df t value
## (Intercept)                             0.27443    0.24618  30.71243   1.115
## TimePeriodMid                           2.01003    0.20923 107.99993   9.607
## TimePeriodLate                          2.24413    0.20923 107.99994  10.726
## GrazingTreatmentCattle                  0.54023    0.30588  30.71243   1.766
## GrazingTreatmentNone                    0.32684    0.30588  30.71243   1.069
## YrsSinceBurn1                           0.12348    0.27668  30.71243   0.446
## YrsSinceBurn2                          -0.06526    0.39668  30.71243  -0.165
## TimePeriodMid:GrazingTreatmentCattle   -0.94793    0.25997 107.99993  -3.646
## TimePeriodLate:GrazingTreatmentCattle  -0.59262    0.25997 107.99993  -2.280
## TimePeriodMid:GrazingTreatmentNone     -0.80569    0.25997 107.99993  -3.099
## TimePeriodLate:GrazingTreatmentNone    -0.54445    0.25997 107.99993  -2.094
## TimePeriodMid:YrsSinceBurn1            -0.56079    0.23515 107.99993  -2.385
## TimePeriodLate:YrsSinceBurn1           -0.92782    0.23515 107.99993  -3.946
## TimePeriodMid:YrsSinceBurn2            -0.83743    0.33714 107.99993  -2.484
## TimePeriodLate:YrsSinceBurn2           -1.02476    0.33714 107.99993  -3.040
##                                       Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                           0.273598    
## TimePeriodMid                         3.65e-16 ***
## TimePeriodLate                         < 2e-16 ***
## GrazingTreatmentCattle                0.087309 .  
## GrazingTreatmentNone                  0.293615    
## YrsSinceBurn1                         0.658509    
## YrsSinceBurn2                         0.870404    
## TimePeriodMid:GrazingTreatmentCattle  0.000411 ***
## TimePeriodLate:GrazingTreatmentCattle 0.024596 *  
## TimePeriodMid:GrazingTreatmentNone    0.002475 ** 
## TimePeriodLate:GrazingTreatmentNone   0.038574 *  
## TimePeriodMid:YrsSinceBurn1           0.018830 *  
## TimePeriodLate:YrsSinceBurn1          0.000142 ***
## TimePeriodMid:YrsSinceBurn2           0.014531 *  
## TimePeriodLate:YrsSinceBurn2          0.002972 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Correlation matrix not shown by default, as p = 15 > 12.
## Use print(x, correlation=TRUE)  or
##     vcov(x)        if you need it
### AIC COMPARISON ------------------------
prediction_i_R_models <- list(mod_Food_01, mod_Food_02)
prediction_i_R_names <- c("by_TP_GT_YB", "by_TP_GT_YB_TPGT_TPYB")
aictab(cand.set = prediction_i_R_models, modnames = prediction_i_R_names)
## 
## Model selection based on AICc:
## 
##                        K   AICc Delta_AICc AICcWt Cum.Wt      LL
## by_TP_GT_YB_TPGT_TPYB 18 311.09       0.00      1      1 -135.15
## by_TP_GT_YB           10 330.14      19.05      0      1 -154.34
### R square results ------------------------
r2_nakagawa(mod_Food_02)
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: 0.735
##      Marginal R2: 0.566

PREDICTION I FIGURE

Bring in data and set up dataframe to use for predictions ii-iv.

# BRING IN DATA  ------------------------
df_GRSP_Food <- read.csv("GRSP_Food_Manuscript_Final_Dec2025.csv")

# ADD IN NAs  ------------------------

is.na(df_GRSP_Food) <- df_GRSP_Food == -999
is.na(df_GRSP_Food) <- df_GRSP_Food == -9

# ASSIGN VARIABLES STRUCTURE AS NEEDED ------------------------
df_GRSP_Food$PlotName<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$PlotName)
df_GRSP_Food$Recap<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$Recap)
df_GRSP_Food$Age<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$Age)
df_GRSP_Food$Sex<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$Sex)
df_GRSP_Food$BurnTreatment<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$BurnTreatment)
df_GRSP_Food$GrazingTreatment<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$GrazingTreatment)
df_GRSP_Food$CaptureYear<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$CaptureYear)
df_GRSP_Food$yearSinceBurn<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$yearSinceBurn)

df_GRSP_Food$FatScoreOrd<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Food$FatScore)
df_GRSP_Food$timeperiod<-factor(df_GRSP_Food$timeperiod, levels = c("Early", "Mid", "Late"))

# SURVEY DATE AS A DATE ------------------------
df_GRSP_Food$CaptureDate <- as.Date(df_GRSP_Food$CaptureDate)

# CAPTURE TIME  ------------------------
df_GRSP_Food$CaptureTimeCST <- as.POSIXct(df_GRSP_Food$CaptureTime, tz = "America/Chicago", format = "%H:%M")
#str(df_GRSP_Food)


# PLOT TYPE ------------------------
# Some plots are not relevant for predictions ii-iV need to be removed.

df_GRSP_Food<-subset(df_GRSP_Food, df_GRSP_Food$PlotName !="4A" & df_GRSP_Food$PlotName !="N20A" & 
                       df_GRSP_Food$PlotName !="BFE" & df_GRSP_Food$PlotName !="NT")

PREDICTION II ANALYSIS

Prediction II: Fueling rates, would be positively associated with the spatial and temporal abundance of prey on the landscape.

##########################################################
######################### STEP 1 #########################
##########################################################

# SUBSET DATA AS NEEDED  ------------------------
# Subset data for 2014, TRIG = FALSE, adults only
df_GRSP_Food_2014<-df_GRSP_Food[df_GRSP_Food$CaptureYear=="2014",]
df_GRSP_Food_2014<-df_GRSP_Food_2014[df_GRSP_Food_2014$TRIG.issues == "FALSE",]
df_GRSP_Food_2014<-df_GRSP_Food_2014[df_GRSP_Food_2014$Age !="HY",]

# CLEAN DATASET AND TRANSFORMATIONS ------------------------

# Dropping outliers and passing back to the dataframe
df_GRSP_Food_2014_02<-df_GRSP_Food_2014[-c(143,39),]

# Incorporate transformation 
df_GRSP_Food_2014_02$ConcTRIG_trans<-(log(df_GRSP_Food_2014_02$ConcTRIG))
df_GRSP_Food_2014_02$MeanEdibleMass_trans<-(log(df_GRSP_Food_2014_02$MeanEdibleMass))

##########################################################
######################### STEP 2  ########################
##########################################################
# Here we are going to look at edible biomass as a predictor of TRIG 

# Mod_01: punitive model  ------------------------
mod_TRIGfood_01 <- lm(ConcTRIG_trans~ CaptureTimeCST + CaptureDate + FatScoreOrd + Sex, data = df_GRSP_Food_2014_02)
summary(mod_TRIGfood_01)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = ConcTRIG_trans ~ CaptureTimeCST + CaptureDate + 
##     FatScoreOrd + Sex, data = df_GRSP_Food_2014_02)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.03421 -0.19198 -0.00135  0.17992  0.76397 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                  Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)    -3.438e+04  4.977e+03  -6.908 4.73e-11 ***
## CaptureTimeCST  1.936e-05  2.808e-06   6.896 5.06e-11 ***
## CaptureDate     3.438e-03  7.674e-04   4.480 1.18e-05 ***
## FatScoreOrd0.5  3.721e-02  7.455e-02   0.499  0.61811    
## FatScoreOrd1    3.973e-02  4.598e-02   0.864  0.38854    
## FatScoreOrd1.5 -2.917e-01  1.525e-01  -1.913  0.05704 .  
## FatScoreOrd2   -5.286e-02  7.177e-02  -0.736  0.46220    
## FatScoreOrd3    1.363e-01  1.163e-01   1.172  0.24238    
## FatScoreOrd4    3.583e-01  1.351e-01   2.652  0.00855 ** 
## SexM           -2.030e-01  4.976e-02  -4.079 6.22e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.2939 on 231 degrees of freedom
##   (68 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3705, Adjusted R-squared:  0.346 
## F-statistic: 15.11 on 9 and 231 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
# Mod_02: with food ------------------------
mod_TRIGfood_02 <- lm(ConcTRIG_trans~ MeanEdibleMass_trans + CaptureTimeCST + CaptureDate + FatScoreOrd + Sex, 
                      data = df_GRSP_Food_2014_02)
summary(mod_TRIGfood_02)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = ConcTRIG_trans ~ MeanEdibleMass_trans + CaptureTimeCST + 
##     CaptureDate + FatScoreOrd + Sex, data = df_GRSP_Food_2014_02)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.0460 -0.1915 -0.0039  0.1835  0.7616 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                        Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)          -3.477e+04  5.013e+03  -6.936 4.15e-11 ***
## MeanEdibleMass_trans -4.263e-03  3.741e-02  -0.114 0.909374    
## CaptureTimeCST        1.959e-05  2.828e-06   6.925 4.43e-11 ***
## CaptureDate           3.446e-03  9.651e-04   3.571 0.000435 ***
## FatScoreOrd0.5        4.067e-02  7.532e-02   0.540 0.589764    
## FatScoreOrd1          4.234e-02  4.639e-02   0.913 0.362365    
## FatScoreOrd1.5       -2.913e-01  1.535e-01  -1.898 0.058967 .  
## FatScoreOrd2         -4.231e-02  7.324e-02  -0.578 0.564006    
## FatScoreOrd3          1.355e-01  1.173e-01   1.155 0.249322    
## FatScoreOrd4          3.569e-01  1.381e-01   2.585 0.010357 *  
## SexM                 -2.089e-01  5.047e-02  -4.138 4.93e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.2953 on 227 degrees of freedom
##   (71 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3704, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3426 
## F-statistic: 13.35 on 10 and 227 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16

PREDICTION II FIGURE

## Warning: Removed 3 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

PREDICTION III ANALYSIS

Prediction III: Territory densities would be positively associated with fueling rates

##########################################################
######################### STEP 1 #########################
##########################################################

# SUBSET DATA AS NEEDED  ------------------------
# Subset data for just males, adults only, TRIG

df_GRSP_Food_Males<-df_GRSP_Food[df_GRSP_Food$Sex=="M",]
df_GRSP_Food_Males<-df_GRSP_Food_Males[df_GRSP_Food_Males$Age !="HY",]
df_GRSP_Food_Males<-df_GRSP_Food_Males[df_GRSP_Food_Males$TRIG.issues == "FALSE",]

##########################################################
######################### STEP 2a ########################
##########################################################
# Determining model for residual TRIG 

# CLEAN DATASET AND TRANSFORM VARIABLES AS NEEDED ------------------------

# Incorporate transformation 
df_GRSP_Food_Males$ConcTRIG_trans<-(log(df_GRSP_Food_Males$ConcTRIG))

# MODELS FOR DETERIMING RESIDUAL TRIG  ------------------------

# Mod_01_resid: with Fat Score ------------------------
mod_TRIGresidMales_01<-lm(ConcTRIG_trans~CaptureTimeCST + CaptureYear + FatScoreOrd, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males)
summary(mod_TRIGresidMales_01)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = ConcTRIG_trans ~ CaptureTimeCST + CaptureYear + 
##     FatScoreOrd, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.56062 -0.20365  0.01347  0.22307  0.79975 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)     -3.939e+04  3.672e+03 -10.728   <2e-16 ***
## CaptureTimeCST   2.222e-05  2.072e-06  10.728   <2e-16 ***
## CaptureYear2014  3.655e-01  3.676e-02   9.941   <2e-16 ***
## CaptureYear2015  5.862e-02  3.698e-02   1.585   0.1136    
## FatScoreOrd0.5  -1.892e-02  5.961e-02  -0.317   0.7511    
## FatScoreOrd1     1.933e-02  3.224e-02   0.599   0.5492    
## FatScoreOrd1.5  -1.871e-01  2.248e-01  -0.832   0.4057    
## FatScoreOrd2     4.490e-02  6.245e-02   0.719   0.4725    
## FatScoreOrd3     2.205e-01  1.311e-01   1.682   0.0931 .  
## FatScoreOrd4     2.410e-01  1.605e-01   1.501   0.1339    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.3164 on 485 degrees of freedom
##   (166 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3676, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3559 
## F-statistic: 31.33 on 9 and 485 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
# Mod_02_resid: without Fat Score ------------------------
mod_TRIGresidMales_02<-lm(ConcTRIG_trans~CaptureTimeCST + CaptureYear, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males)
summary(mod_TRIGresidMales_02)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = ConcTRIG_trans ~ CaptureTimeCST + CaptureYear, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.56910 -0.19968  0.01309  0.21868  0.80514 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)     -4.016e+04  3.612e+03 -11.119   <2e-16 ***
## CaptureTimeCST   2.266e-05  2.038e-06  11.119   <2e-16 ***
## CaptureYear2014  3.698e-01  3.612e-02  10.237   <2e-16 ***
## CaptureYear2015  5.309e-02  3.646e-02   1.456    0.146    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.316 on 493 degrees of freedom
##   (164 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3593, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3554 
## F-statistic: 92.15 on 3 and 493 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
##########################################################
######################### STEP 2b ########################
##########################################################

### EXTRACT RESIDUALS ------------------------
# Extracting residuals from best fit model: mod_TRIGcovarMales_02
mod_TRIGresidMales_02_Nas<-lm(ConcTRIG_trans~CaptureTimeCST + CaptureYear, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males, na.action = na.exclude)
df_MaleResid02_Nas<-data.frame(resid(mod_TRIGresidMales_02_Nas))

# Rename the column and then merge dataframes based on row number
names(df_MaleResid02_Nas)[1] <- "MaleResid_mod02"
df_GRSP_Food_Males_resid02<-merge(df_GRSP_Food_Males, df_MaleResid02_Nas, by = 0)

# Rename for ease ------------------------
df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids<-df_GRSP_Food_Males_resid02

##########################################################
######################### STEP 3a ########################
##########################################################

# SUMMARIZE  ------------------------
# Summarize so that there is one residual values and time period per density measure. 
df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_sub<-select(df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids, c(2:10, 17, 20, 25))
df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_02<-df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_sub[complete.cases(df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_sub), ]

df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03 <- df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_02 %>%
  group_by(PlotName, timeperiod) %>%
   summarize(MeanDensity = mean(MeanDensity),
             MeanMaleResid_mod02 = mean(MaleResid_mod02))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PlotName'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
# TRANSFORM VARIABLES AS NEEDED  ------------------------

# Incorporate transformation 
df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03$MeanDensity_trans<-(sqrt(df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03$MeanDensity))

##########################################################
######################### STEP 3b ########################
##########################################################

#### MODELS FOR PREDICTION III  ------------------------

# Model 01: no covariates  ------------------------
mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_01 <- lm(MeanDensity ~ MeanMaleResid_mod02, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03)
summary(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_01) 
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = MeanDensity ~ MeanMaleResid_mod02, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.2799 -1.8858 -0.5373  1.4361  7.7809 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                     Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)           3.1283     0.3992   7.836 1.12e-09 ***
## MeanMaleResid_mod02  -1.6707     2.3935  -0.698    0.489    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.586 on 41 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.01174,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.01236 
## F-statistic: 0.4872 on 1 and 41 DF,  p-value: 0.4891
# Model 02: with time  ------------------------
mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_02 <- lm(MeanDensity ~ MeanMaleResid_mod02 + timeperiod, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03)
summary(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_02)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = MeanDensity ~ MeanMaleResid_mod02 + timeperiod, 
##     data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.2664 -1.7236 -0.4833  1.3590  7.8741 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                     Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)           3.4927     0.6630   5.268 5.36e-06 ***
## MeanMaleResid_mod02  -1.5876     2.5014  -0.635    0.529    
## timeperiodMid        -0.4697     0.9729  -0.483    0.632    
## timeperiodLate       -0.7065     0.9881  -0.715    0.479    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.633 on 39 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.02541,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.04955 
## F-statistic: 0.339 on 3 and 39 DF,  p-value: 0.7972
# Model 03: with interaction  ------------------------
mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_03 <- lm(MeanDensity ~ MeanMaleResid_mod02 + timeperiod + MeanMaleResid_mod02*timeperiod, 
                               data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03)
summary(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_03)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = MeanDensity ~ MeanMaleResid_mod02 + timeperiod + 
##     MeanMaleResid_mod02 * timeperiod, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_resids_03)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -4.0965 -1.5631 -0.5472  1.3374  7.0834 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                    Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)                          3.6210     0.6649   5.446 3.54e-06 ***
## MeanMaleResid_mod02                 -5.6585     4.1253  -1.372    0.178    
## timeperiodMid                       -0.4933     0.9670  -0.510    0.613    
## timeperiodLate                      -0.6553     1.0536  -0.622    0.538    
## MeanMaleResid_mod02:timeperiodMid    8.7969     5.6085   1.568    0.125    
## MeanMaleResid_mod02:timeperiodLate   1.5424     6.7611   0.228    0.821    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.608 on 37 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.09269,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.02992 
## F-statistic: 0.756 on 5 and 37 DF,  p-value: 0.5872
### AIC COMPARISON ------------------------
# Define list of models for AIC comparison, vector with names
# Then calculate AIC for each model
prediction_iii_models <- list(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_01, mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_02, mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_03)
prediction_iii_names <- c("by_TRIG", "by_TRIG_TP", "by_TRIG_TP_INTERACTION")
aictab(cand.set = prediction_iii_models, modnames = prediction_iii_names)
## 
## Model selection based on AICc:
## 
##                        K   AICc Delta_AICc AICcWt Cum.Wt      LL
## by_TRIG                3 208.31       0.00   0.88   0.88 -100.85
## by_TRIG_TP             5 212.71       4.41   0.10   0.97 -100.55
## by_TRIG_TP_INTERACTION 7 215.22       6.91   0.03   1.00  -99.01
### ADDITIONAL BEST MODEL FINDINGS ------------------------
# Model 1, summary above
AIC(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_01)
## [1] 207.6905
logLik(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_01)
## 'log Lik.' -100.8452 (df=3)
df.residual(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_01) # But also in summary output
## [1] 41
deviance(mod_TRIG_MalesDensity_01)
## [1] 274.1839

PREDICTION III FIGURE

PREDICTION IV ANALYSIS

Prediction IV: Territory densities would be positively associated with the abundance of potential prey.

##########################################################
######################### STEP 1 #########################
##########################################################

# SUBSET DATA AS NEEDED  ------------------------
# Just adult males, using df_GRSP_Food_Males from above.
df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014<-df_GRSP_Food_Males[df_GRSP_Food_Males$CaptureYear=="2014",]

# SUMMARIZE  ------------------------
# Summarize so that there is one ediable biomass values and time period per density measure. 
df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_02<-select(df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014, c(1:7, 16, 19:20))
df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_03<-df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_02[complete.cases(df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_02), ]

df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04 <- df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_03 %>%
  group_by(PlotName, timeperiod) %>%
   summarize(MeanDensity = mean(MeanDensity),
             MeanEdibleMass = mean(MeanEdibleMass))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'PlotName'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
# TRANSFORMING VARIABLES AS NEEDED ------------------------

# Incorporate transformation. 
df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04$MeanEdibleMass_trans<-(log(df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04$MeanEdibleMass))


##########################################################
######################### STEP 2 #########################
##########################################################

# Mod_01: no covariates ------------------------
mod_denseFood_01 <- lm(MeanDensity ~ MeanEdibleMass_trans, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04)
summary(mod_denseFood_01)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = MeanDensity ~ MeanEdibleMass_trans, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.9538 -1.9347 -0.1954  1.8800  4.2278 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                      Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)           3.92988    1.00347   3.916 0.000552 ***
## MeanEdibleMass_trans  0.01101    0.58767   0.019 0.985188    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.334 on 27 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  1.3e-05,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.03702 
## F-statistic: 0.0003511 on 1 and 27 DF,  p-value: 0.9852
# Mod_02 with time ------------------------
mod_denseFood_02 <- lm(MeanDensity ~ MeanEdibleMass_trans + timeperiod, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04)
summary(mod_denseFood_02)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = MeanDensity ~ MeanEdibleMass_trans + timeperiod, 
##     data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.9582 -1.4079 -0.2292  1.9043  3.8578 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                      Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)            4.1604     1.0557   3.941 0.000576 ***
## MeanEdibleMass_trans   0.4494     0.7349   0.612 0.546337    
## timeperiodMid         -1.1774     1.2474  -0.944 0.354293    
## timeperiodLate        -1.3317     1.3721  -0.971 0.341092    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.374 on 25 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.04189,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.07308 
## F-statistic: 0.3644 on 3 and 25 DF,  p-value: 0.7793
# Mod_03 with interactions ------------------------
mod_denseFood_03 <- lm(MeanDensity ~ MeanEdibleMass_trans + timeperiod + MeanEdibleMass_trans*timeperiod, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04)
summary(mod_denseFood_03)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = MeanDensity ~ MeanEdibleMass_trans + timeperiod + 
##     MeanEdibleMass_trans * timeperiod, data = df_GRSP_Food_Males_2014_04)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.6276 -1.3448 -0.3109  1.5804  4.0169 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                     Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
## (Intercept)                          4.59926    1.51542   3.035  0.00589 **
## MeanEdibleMass_trans                -0.05413    1.43913  -0.038  0.97032   
## timeperiodMid                       -0.52746    2.56817  -0.205  0.83908   
## timeperiodLate                      -4.16716    3.04922  -1.367  0.18495   
## MeanEdibleMass_trans:timeperiodMid  -0.15060    1.85289  -0.081  0.93592   
## MeanEdibleMass_trans:timeperiodLate  1.73992    1.94426   0.895  0.38011   
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2.406 on 23 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.09431,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.1026 
## F-statistic: 0.479 on 5 and 23 DF,  p-value: 0.7882
### AIC COMPARISON ------------------------
# Define list of models for AIC comparison, vector with names
# Then calculate AIC for each model
# Best model is mod_denseFood_01 - just ediable biomass. 
prediction_iv_models<- list(mod_denseFood_01, mod_denseFood_02, mod_denseFood_03)
prediction_iv_names<- c("by_EdBio", "by_EdBio_TP", "by_EdBio_TP_EdBioTP")
aictab(cand.set = prediction_iv_models, modnames = prediction_iv_names)
## 
## Model selection based on AICc:
## 
##                     K   AICc Delta_AICc AICcWt Cum.Wt     LL
## by_EdBio            3 136.34       0.00   0.89   0.89 -64.69
## by_EdBio_TP         5 140.74       4.41   0.10   0.99 -64.07
## by_EdBio_TP_EdBioTP 7 145.84       9.50   0.01   1.00 -63.25
### ADDITIONAL BEST MODEL FINDINGS ------------------------
# Model 1, summary above
AIC(mod_denseFood_01)
## [1] 135.3751
logLik(mod_denseFood_01)
## 'log Lik.' -64.68756 (df=3)
df.residual(mod_denseFood_01)
## [1] 27
deviance(mod_denseFood_01)
## [1] 147.029

PREDICTION IV FIGURE

PREDICTION V ANALYSIS

Prediction V: Fueling rates would increase from first to second territories to a greater extent in dispersers than in site-faithful individuals.

##########################################################
######################### STEP 1 #########################
##########################################################

# BRING IN DATA  ------------------------
df_GRSP_Dispersal <- read.csv("GRSP_DispersalData_withBFE_April2025.csv")

# ASSIGN VARIABLES STRUCTURE AS NEEDED ------------------------
df_GRSP_Dispersal$ColorBand<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Dispersal$ColorBand)
df_GRSP_Dispersal$DispersalStatus<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Dispersal$DispersalStatus)
df_GRSP_Dispersal$Year<-as.factor(df_GRSP_Dispersal$Year)

# TRANSFORMING VARIABLES AS NEEDED ------------------------
# None needed. 


##########################################################
######################### STEP 2 #########################
##########################################################

# T-TestWITH Diff_ResidTRIG ------------------------
# One sided asking if disperses have greater ResidTRIG than the non-disperses
mod_Diff_ResidTRIG_01<-t.test(Diff_ResidTRIG~DispersalStatus, data = df_GRSP_Dispersal, var.equal = TRUE, alternative = "greater")
mod_Diff_ResidTRIG_01
## 
##  Two Sample t-test
## 
## data:  Diff_ResidTRIG by DispersalStatus
## t = 0.21419, df = 21, p-value = 0.4162
## alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group disperser and group non-disperser is greater than 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.22408      Inf
## sample estimates:
##     mean in group disperser mean in group non-disperser 
##                  0.08855656                  0.05669799

PREDICTION V FIGURE
